phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. The Story is persisted and `agent::ready`.

## Summary

Ran the `/plan` pipeline headlessly (interrogate → author → critics → persist). No delivery was performed, per instructions.

**Story #1 — JSON REST API for user accounts and personal notes with persistent store**
- URL: https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-23-story-scope-mandrel-dd97c6f3/issues/1
- State: `agent::ready` · cohort label `plan-run::aa70e1bc` · wave 0

**Key decisions:**
- **Single Story.** Route computed as `full` only because the seed exceeds 150 words — but this is one cohesive, restart-durable capability whose auth, persistence, and endpoints aren't reviewable in isolation, so the default-single policy holds.
- **Zero-dependency approach.** Contract-level Spec pins the load-bearing constraints (built-in `node:http`, no external framework, file-backed on-disk store such as Node 24's built-in `node:sqlite` — in-memory is disqualifying) while leaving implementation to the deliverer.
- **Security invariants surfaced** from `rules/security-baseline.md`: hashed passwords never echoed, unguessable opaque sessions, per-user note isolation, 400s on bad input without crashing.
- 11 observable acceptance criteria (including restart persistence and per-user isolation); verify via `npm test` (contract), `npm run typecheck` / `npm run lint` (validate).

**Gates:** both fresh-context critics skipped (ledgered — draft too small / under budget); text-hygiene clean; dry-run passed all validator gates before the live write.

Deliver later with `/deliver 1` (not run in this session).

Story #1 delivered and landed. Relaying the terminal envelope.

## Delivery complete — Story #1 `landed`

`/deliver 1 --yes` ran the single-Story delivery engine end-to-end. Terminal status: **`landed`**.

| | |
|---|---|
| **PR** | [#2](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-23-story-scope-mandrel-dd97c6f3/pull/2) — **MERGED** (squash), checks `success` |
| **Branch** | `story-1` → `main` |
| **Gates** | validation ✅ · base-sync ✅ · code-review ✅ (critical 0 / high 0 / medium 0) |
| **Acceptance self-eval** | 11/11 criteria `met` → `proceed` (fresh-context critic) |
| **Label** | `agent::done` (merge confirmed) |

**What shipped** (dependency-free, ESM, no web framework):
- `src/store.js` — file-backed `node:sqlite` store; scrypt-hashed passwords (per-user salt, never persisted plaintext, never echoed), unguessable 32-byte random session tokens, parameterized queries.
- `src/server.js` — `node:http` handler for `POST /signup` (201, 409 on duplicate), `POST /login` (200 `{session}`, 401 on bad creds), `GET /me`, `POST /notes`, `GET /notes` — the last three gated on `Authorization: Bearer`. Malformed/empty/wrong-Content-Type bodies → 400 JSON; unhandled errors → 500 without leaking detail or crashing. Binds `PORT` (default 3000).
- `test/api.test.js` — `node --test` suite covering all 11 ACs including per-user isolation and restart persistence; exits 0.
- `package.json` — added `"start": "node src/server.js"`; `.gitignore` excludes the runtime `data/` store.

**One degraded, non-blocking tail note:** the post-land fast-forward of local `main` was skipped because the working tree is dirty (the pre-existing untracked `temp/`). The merge stands on the remote — this only means your local `main` wasn't auto-advanced; `git pull` when convenient.

One design note worth surfacing: the security baseline's default session guidance (httpOnly cookies) was superseded here by the Story's explicit contract for an opaque **bearer token** API — the narrower spec governs. The inviolable password/secret MUSTs (hashing, no plaintext, no hardcoded secrets) are all honored.

tool_input.command: cd "/private/var/folders/9_/_b_wsjg97pz469z8wzf02hhc0000gn/T/bench-sbx-46QYpw/.worktrees/story-1" && PORT=39117 DB_PATH="$PWD/temp-smoke/app.db" node src/server.js & SRV=$!; sleep 1.5; echo "--- signup ---"; curl -s -XPOST localhost:39117/signup -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"username":"smoke","password":"pw"}'; echo; echo "--- login ---"; S=$(curl -s -XPOST localhost:39117/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"username":"smoke","password":"pw"}' | node -e 'process.stdin.on("data",d=>console.log(JSON.parse(d).session))'); echo "session=$S"; echo "--- me ---"; curl -s localhost:39117/me -H "Authorization: Bearer $S"; echo; kill $SRV; rm -rf temp-smoke
tool_input.description: Smoke test npm start with PORT env
